rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (plan mode, connectors).
Sessions are the unit of work.
No as-is imports from the internet.
Delegation boundaries — student brings, Claude screens.
Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file must exist and be available.
Forcing functions stay; scripted reactions go — and forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
No placeholders inside fenced blocks; open-hook pattern for student-supplied input, all inside ONE fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 179-183: P9 close prompt ends at line 180 fence-close; 'Then the question.' is body prose on line 183, outside the fence. Student question is sent as a separate message rather than appended after the colon inside the fence — not proper open-hook.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Restructure P9 as proper open-hook: colon stays inside fence as last line, body prose instructs student to append question and send once.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
Question-dump default — 'in turn' is too soft; write one-at-a-time structurally.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific).
Source URL + type label — mandatory for every claim.
Zombie stat guard — round-number AI failure rates.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude Code CLI reads any path you name. For sources outside the training folder, give Claude the absolute path; it reads the file directly.
Claude Code Desktop reads files you attach via the + button at the prompt. For sources outside the training folder, attach them with + before sending the prompt.
Cowork reads files in the working folders you've selected for this task. Add another folder (your downloads, a notes directory) via the + button.

Turn on plan mode first. Tell Claude "Enable plan mode." (Alternatives: pick Plan from the mode dropdown at the bottom of the Claude Code desktop app, or press Shift+Tab to cycle.) The footer should read plan mode.
Claude will pause and ask "Claude has written up a plan and is ready to execute. Would you like to proceed?" with four options. Option 3 = Ultraplan on web.
Plan mode exits automatically after an approved plan runs, so the footer is already back to default.

todos.description: Line 65: Ultraplan reference (option 3) in the four-options dialog — marked N/A in body prose ('aren't needed here') but the name still appears. Prior REVISE finding, non-blocking carry-over per re-audit context.

Line 179-183: P9 close prompt — student question lives outside the fence as body prose instruction ('Then the question.') rather than as a proper open-hook colon-inside-fence. Non-blocking; two-step still works mechanically.
